is a Namibian [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club based in [Okahandja](/wiki/Okahandja "Okahandja"). The club currently competes in the [Namibia Premier Football League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League"), the top level of [football in Namibia](/wiki/Football_in_Namibia "Football in Namibia").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Namibian football club

Football club

Okahandja United

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Full name | Okahandja United Football Club |
| Founded | 2015 (as Military School FC) |
| Ground | [Nau-AIB Stadium](/w/index.php?title=Nau-AIB_Stadium&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nau-AIB Stadium (page does not exist)"), [Okahandja](/wiki/Okahandja "Okahandja") |
| Chairman | [Johannes Hindjou](/wiki/Johannes_Hindjou "Johannes Hindjou") |
| Manager | [Richard Gariseb](/wiki/Richard_Gariseb "Richard Gariseb")[[1]](#cite_note-gap_at_the_top-1) |
| League | [Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership") |
| [2023–24](/wiki/2023%E2%80%9324_Namibia_Premiership "2023–24 Namibia Premiership") | 8th |
| Website | <https://www.facebook.com/okunitedfc/> |
|  | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | **[Home colours](/wiki/Kit_(association_football) "Kit (association football)")** | **[Away colours](/wiki/Away_colours "Away colours")** | | |
|  | |

**Okahandja United FC** is a Namibian [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club based in [Okahandja](/wiki/Okahandja "Okahandja"). The club currently competes in the [Namibia Premier Football League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League"), the top level of [football in Namibia](/wiki/Football_in_Namibia "Football in Namibia").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja_United_FC&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In 2018, under its original name of Okahandja Military School FC, the club defeated [African Motto FC](/w/index.php?title=African_Motto_FC&action=edit&redlink=1 "African Motto FC (page does not exist)") from [Oshakati](/wiki/Oshakati "Oshakati") in the playoffs to be promoted to the [Namibia Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_League "Namibia Premier League"). In mid-September 2018, the club was purchased by former [Namibian international](/wiki/Namibia_national_football_team "Namibia national football team") [Johannes Hindjou](/wiki/Johannes_Hindjou "Johannes Hindjou") and given its current name. [[2]](#cite_note-Hindjou_takes_over-2) Originally, the club was denied promotion to the Premier League because previously the club was owned by the [Namibian Defence Force](/wiki/Namibian_Defence_Force "Namibian Defence Force"), the same ownership as another NPL club, [Mighty Gunners](/wiki/Mighty_Gunners_F.C. "Mighty Gunners F.C."). After the club was sold to Hindjou, it was in compliance with the [Namibia Football Association](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Association "Namibia Football Association")'s league statutes and was cleared to participate.[[3]](#cite_note-Okahandja_United_Joins_Big_Guns-3) Following the change in ownership, veteran Namibia coach Woody Jacobs was named manager.[[4]](#cite_note-Jacobs_as_New_Coach-4)

Further controversy occurred the following month when sixteen players who helped win promotion were released by the club. While the players stated it was because they asked for a bonus they were promised, the club maintained that the new manager Jacobs was bringing in his own players who he felt were capable of competing in the top flight.[[5]](#cite_note-Cleans-5) Just a week later, another bombshell revelation occurred when the Namibian Defense Force sent a letter to the NFA stating that they were still the legal owners of the club because they were not informed and did not consent to the sale.[[6]](#cite_note-serious_scrutiny-6) The letter was sent despite the first letter notifying the NFA being signed by the [brigadier-general](/wiki/Brigadier-general "Brigadier-general") of the military school.[[7]](#cite_note-Saga_Threatens_NPL-7) Local media believed the NDA tried to go back on the deal after Hindjou stated that the former owners of Military School FC owned the former players bonuses, not him.[[8]](#cite_note-Military_School_Saga-8)

After football leagues were cancelled in Namibia for several seasons, mostly because of a dispute between the NFA and the [Namibia Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_League "Namibia Premier League"), Okahandja United FC was part of the inaugural campaign of the newly-formed [Namibia Premier Football League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League") for the [2022–23 season](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_Namibia_Premier_Football_League "2022–23 Namibia Premier Football League"). The club won its first-ever top-flight match 2–1 on matchday one at their home [Nau-AIB Stadium](/w/index.php?title=Nau-AIB_Stadium&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nau-AIB Stadium (page does not exist)") against Mighty Gunners. The club was lead in the campaign by head coach [Richard Gariseb](/wiki/Richard_Gariseb "Richard Gariseb") and captained by [Stanley Scheeffers](/w/index.php?title=Stanley_Scheeffers&action=edit&redlink=1 "Stanley Scheeffers (page does not exist)").[[9]](#cite_note-Make_it_difficult-9) Following matchday three, Okahandja United sat atop the league table with seven points.[[10]](#cite_note-perfect_start-10)

## Performance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja_United_FC&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Performance")]

| Season | League | Place |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2017/18[[11]](#cite_note-Namibia_2017/18-11) | [First Division](/wiki/Namibia_First_Division "Namibia First Division") (North-East) | 1st |
| [2018/19](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_Namibia_Premier_League "2018–19 Namibia Premier League")[[12]](#cite_note-Namibia_2018/19-12) | [Namibia Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_League "Namibia Premier League") | 12th |
| 2019/20 | No League |  |
| 2020/21 | No League |  |
| 2021/22 | No League |  |
| [2022/23](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_Namibia_Premier_Football_League "2022–23 Namibia Premier Football League")[[13]](#cite_note-Namibia_2022/23-13) | [Namibia Premier Football League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League") | 6th |

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja_United_FC&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Honours")]

* [First Division](/wiki/Namibia_First_Division "Namibia First Division") (North-East)

:   :   **Champions**: 2018/19
